prompt: Why does this code fail to compile?
error_message: error[E0505]: cannot move out of `s` because it is borrowed
explanation: Calling drop(s) moves s while r still holds a borrow of it. Removing the drop allows r to be used safely before s is freed at end of scope.

prompt: Why does this code fail to compile? The value seems to still be in scope.
error_message: error[E0597]: `c` does not live long enough
explanation: Dropping c before using r1 causes the borrow to outlive the value. Moving or dropping c invalidates all references, so removing drop(c) allows r1 to be used safely.

prompt: Why does this async function panic at runtime? It seems to borrow data after it was freed.
explanation: Dropping nums frees the memory before the borrow is used. Removing drop(nums) allows the borrow to be valid until end of scope.

prompt: Why does this code fail to compile? The iterator borrows the value after it has been moved.
error_message: error[E0507]: cannot move out of `*i` because it is borrowed
explanation: The closure inside map moves the value that is still borrowed by the iterator. Using iter() instead of into_iter() prevents moving the values.

prompt: Why does this code fail to compile? The assignment inside the loop causes an ownership error.
error_message: error[E0506]: cannot assign to `v` because it is borrowed
explanation: The loop borrows elements of v immutably, but attempts to mutate v. Moving the push operation outside the borrow scope resolves this.
